title: Estimate to divide by 1-digit numbers
description: Practice using arrays and number lines to estimate dividing by 1-digit numbers.
topic_path: Topic E: Division of tens and ones with successive remainders

assessment_items.question_content:
The following array represents $68\div 9$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What two whole numbers is the quotient between?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can use the related multiplication equation to help us.

$\goldE{68} \div \blueE{9} = \maroonE{{?}}$

So, $\blueE{9} \times \maroonE{{?}}= \goldE{68}$.

No whole number value of $\maroonE{{?}}$ makes the equation exactly true. 

Let's try different values of $\maroonE{?}$ to get close to a product of $\goldE{68}$.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{9} \times \maroonE{{7}} &= 63 \\\\
\blueE{9} \times \maroonE{{8}} &= 72 \\\\
\blueE{9} \times \maroonE{{9}} &= 81
\end{align}$

Which two values of $\maroonE{?}$ get us the closest?

The total $\goldE{68}$ is between $63$ and $72$, so $\maroonE{?}$ is between $\maroonE{7}$ and $\maroonE{8}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

It takes more than $\maroonE{7}$ rows of $\blueE{9}$, but less than $\maroonE{8}$ full rows of $\blueE{9}$ to equal $\goldE{68}$.
The quotient is between $\maroonE{7}$ and $\maroonE{8}$.

options.alt: An array of 68 circles arranged in 7 rows of 9 circles each and 1 row with 5 circles.

assessment_items.question_content:

\blueE{2} \times \maroonE{8} &= 16 \\\\
\blueE{2} \times \maroonE{9} &= 18 \\\\
\blueE{2} \times \maroonE{10} &= 20
\end{align}$

Which two values of $\maroonE{?}$ get us the closest to $\goldE{17}$?

The total $\goldE{17}$ is between $16$ and $18$, so $\maroonE{?}$ is between $\maroonE{8}$ and $\maroonE{9}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

It takes more than $\maroonE{8}$ rows of $\blueE{2}$, but less than $\maroonE{9}$ full rows of $\blueE{2}$ to equal $\goldE{17}$.
The quotient is between $\maroonE{8}$ and $\maroonE{9}$.

options.alt: An array of 17 circles arranged in 8 rows of 2 circles each and 1 row with 1 circle.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following quotient is between two whole numbers.

$38 \div 4$

**Which point on the number line could represent the quotient?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can use the related multiplication equation to help us.

$\goldE{38} \div \blueE{4} = \maroonE{{?}}$

So, $\blueE{4} \times \maroonE{{?}}= \goldE{38}$.

No whole number value of $\maroonE{{?}}$ makes the equation exactly true. 

Let's try different values of $\maroonE{?}$ to get close to a product of $\goldE{38}$.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{4} \times \maroonE{8} &= 32 \\\\
\blueE{4} \times \maroonE{9} &= 36 \\\\
\blueE{4} \times \maroonE{10} &= 40
\end{align}$

Which two values of $\maroonE{?}$ get us the closest to $\goldE{38}$?
The total $\goldE{38}$ is between $36$ and $40$, so $\maroonE{?}$ is between $\maroonE{9}$ and $\maroonE{10}$.

The point that represents $\goldE{38} \div \blueE{4}$ is between $\maroonE{9}$ and $\maroonE{10}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

\blueE{8} \times \maroonE{6} &= 48 \\\\

assessment_items.question_content:
The following array represents $87\div 9$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Compare with $>, <,$ or $=$.**

$87\div9$ [[☃ dropdown 1]] $10$
hints:
No whole number value of $\maroonE{{?}}$ makes this equation exactly true:

$\goldE{87} \div \blueE{9} = \maroonE{{?}}$

Let's try some close equations to estimate the value of $\maroonE{{?}}$.

$87\div9$ is between $\maroonE9$ and $\maroonE{10}$.  So,  it is **less than** $10$.

[[☃ image 1]]

It takes more than $\maroonE{9}$ rows of $\blueE{9}$, but less than $\maroonE{10}$ full rows of $\blueE{9}$ to equal $\goldE{87}$.

options.alt: An array of 87 circles arranged in 9 rows of 9 circles each and 1 row with 6 circles.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which number line correctly compares $\blueE{18\div 5}$ with $\maroonE{5}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Smaller numbers appear further left on the number line and larger numbers appear further right.

Numbers with the same value are in the same place on the number line.

Is the quotient $18\div 5$ less than, equal to, or greater than $5$?

No whole number value of $\blueE{{?}}$ makes this equation exactly true:

$\blueE{18 \div 5} = \blueE{{?}}$

Let's try some close equations to estimate the value of $\blueE{{?}}$.

$\begin{align}
\goldE{15 \div 5} &= \goldE{3}\\\\
\blueE{18 \div 5} &= \blueE{{?}}\\\\
\maroonE{20 \div 5} &= \maroonE{4}
\end{align}$

When the total gets larger and the number of groups is the same, then the group size get larger.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueE{18\div 5}$ is between $\goldE{3}$ and $\maroonE{4}$.  So,  it is *less than* $5$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$18\div 5$ appears to the *left* of $5$ on the number line.

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![An open number line shows a point labeled, "18 divided by 5," to the left of a point labeled, "5."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/64b884c1c54fdc1fbfaf6e4c295762b33fdccac6)
![An open number line shows a point labeled, "18 divided by 5," at the same place as a point labeled, "5."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0a38d3e8c87ffe2e36d5e3853bf56fbb17025989)
![An open number line shows a point labeled, "18 divided by 5," to the right of a point labeled, "5."](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/55dfad89a0823571f11cf23847f38bcb1787b458)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which number line correctly compares $\blueE{36\div 8}$ with $\maroonE{4}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Smaller numbers appear further left on the number line and larger numbers appear further right.

Numbers with the same value are in the same place on the number line.

Is the quotient $36\div 8$ less than, equal to, or greater than $4$?

No whole number value of $\blueE{{?}}$ makes this equation exactly true:

$\blueE{36 \div 8} = \blueE{{?}}$

Let's try some close equations to estimate the value of $\blueE{{?}}$.

$\begin{align}
\goldE{32 \div 8} &= \goldE{4}\\\\
\blueE{36 \div 8} &= \blueE{{?}}\\\\
\maroonE{40 \div 8} &= \maroonE{5}
\end{align}$

When the total gets larger and the number of groups is the same, then the group size get larger.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueE{36\div 8}$ is between $\goldE{4}$ and $\maroonE{5}$.  So,  it is *greater than* $4$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$36\div 8$ appears to the *right* of $4$ on the number line.

[[☃ image 1]]
